sections.paragraph: f. pl. גֶּחָלִים, גַּחֲלֵי (f. Eze 1:13), a live coal (different from פֶּחָם a black coal, Pro 26:21). Job 41:13 Pro 6:28 Isa 44:19, fully גַּחֲלֵי אֵשׁ Lev 16:12. Poet. live coals are used for lightnings, 2Sa 22:9, 13 2Sa 22:13. Hence used of punishments sent by God, Psa 140:11. “Live coals upon the head,” a proverbial expression for any thing very troublesome, which gives any one very great pain and torment. Pro 25:21, “if thine enemy hunger, feed him; if he thirst, give him drink. 22 Pro 25:22… thou wilt heap coals of fire on his head;” i.e. so thou wilt overwhelm him with very heavy cares, and he will be ashamed of his enmity against thee; comp. Rom 12:20. In like manner the Arabs say figuratively “coals of the heart, fire of the liver,” to denote burning cares, and a mind heated and suffused with shame. Compare my remarks on this expression in Rosenmüller, Rep. i. page 140, and in the Lond. Classical Journal, No. 54, p. 244. Elsewhere a live coal, which alone remains to keep in fire, like the Gr. ζώπυρον, denotes the only hope of a race almost destroyed, 2Sa 14:7.
